title: Harden the API Dockerfile (root user, unpinned base, no caching)
description:
# Issue #210 - Container review failed before production

Platform review rejected our API image (starter/Dockerfile):
- FROM python:latest - builds are not reproducible
- the container runs as root
- pip caches wheels inside the image (bloat)
- no WORKDIR, files land in /
- copying the whole context before installing deps busts the layer cache on every code change

**Acceptance criteria:**
- pin the base image to a specific tag (no :latest anywhere)
- create and switch to a non-root USER
- pip installs use --no-cache-dir
- set a WORKDIR
- COPY requirements.txt and install dependencies BEFORE copying the rest of the source
optimal_hint: Ideal: FROM python:3.12-slim, WORKDIR /app, COPY requirements.txt + RUN pip install --no-cache-dir before COPY . ., a dedicated uid/gid user created with groupadd/useradd, USER directive after the COPYs. Bonus polish: EXPOSE kept, CMD unchanged.
